content: {"stances":[{"url":"https://build.inc/insights/data-center-electrical-procurement-sequence-workflow","stance":"supports","quote":"Power transformer lead times from major manufacturers have reached 48-60 months in 2026, with Tier 1 suppliers booked through 2030."},{"url":"https://www.gridreadiness.com/blog/power-transformer-lead-times-ai-data-center-2026","stance":"supports","quote":"in 2026, the lead time for a major HV transformer from a Tier 1 manufacturer has reached 60 months or more."},{"url":"https://www.globaldatacenterhub.com/p/how-to-underwrite-the-hyperscale","stance":"supports","quote":"A full cycle runs 18 to 30 months in favorable markets and 4 to 7 years in constrained jurisdictions."},{"url":"https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17637153","stance":"supports","quote":"Three structural constraints create 7-10 year minimum timeline (not industry-claimed 18-24 months)"},{"url":"https://www.idcpdu.com/news/industry-news/how-to-evaluate-a-reliable-pdu-manufacturer.html","stance":"silent","quote":"What certifications should a reliable PDU manufacturer have?"},{"url":"https://www.upcom.com.tr/blog/rack-pdu-socket-types/","stance":"silent","quote":"the correct answer depends on the equipment cord ecosystem, target market, mounting format and compliance path"}],"status":"corroborated","statusRationale":"Multiple independent industry sources corroborate that power transformer lead times have extended to 48-60+ months in 2026, and that full data center buildout cycles in constrained jurisdictions can stretch to 4-10 years, consistent with the claim being evaluated.","evidence":[],"revisedLikelihood":null,"revisionRationale":null}
